title: Add serialize/deserialize methods to catalog dataclasses
description:
Add Arrow IPC serialization directly to the dataclasses in vgi/catalog/catalog_interface.py.

Add to each dataclass:
- serialize() -> bytes method
- @classmethod deserialize(batch: pa.RecordBatch) -> Self method
- Arrow schema class variable for each type

Dataclasses to update:
- CatalogAttachResult
- SchemaInfo  
- TableInfo
- ViewInfo
- FunctionInfo
- ScanFunctionResult

Serialization rules from plan:
- Single-row batches for scalar returns
- Multi-row batches for streaming (Iterable returns)
- None = 0-row/0-column batch
- Column names match field names exactly
- SerializedSchema fields use pa.binary()
- tags fields use pa.map_(pa.string(), pa.string())

Also create vgi/catalog/__init__.py with package exports.

title: Add is_varargs to ParameterInfo and metadata extraction
description:
In vgi/metadata.py:
- Add is_varargs: bool = False to ParameterInfo
- Update to_dict() and from_dict()
- Add is_varargs field to _PARAMETER_STRUCT for Arrow serialization
- Extract varargs flag in extract_parameters()
- Add _validate_varargs() with rules:
  - Only one varargs parameter allowed
  - Must be positional (not named)
  - Must be last positional (before TableInput if present)
  - Cannot have default value
close_reason: Added is_varargs to ParameterInfo, _PARAMETER_STRUCT, extract_parameters(), and _validate_varargs()

title: Remove redundant InitInputType class attribute
description: InitInputType class attribute duplicates the generic type parameter: 'class ScalarFunctionGenerator(Function[FunctionInitInput])' already specifies the type, but 'InitInputType = FunctionInitInput' repeats it. Investigate using get_type_hints or __orig_bases__ to infer the type and remove the redundant attribute.
close_reason: PR #10 created - uses _get_init_input_type() to infer type from generic parameter

title: Move distributed state management to optional mixin
description: The Function base class in function.py includes ~200 lines for distributed state management (store_state, collect_states, enqueue_work, dequeue_work, work queue storage). Not all functions need this. Extract to DistributedStateMixin that functions can opt into, keeping Function base class simpler for basic use cases.
close_reason: Analysis complete: extraction not recommended. The distributed state methods are tightly coupled with execution_identifier and storage, which are used by core initialization methods. Extraction would require moving initialize_global_state/load_global_state to the mixin, breaking the protocol and requiring multiple inheritance. Current API is already opt-in (just don't call the methods) and well-documented.

title: Add tests for example function Arrow serialization
description:
Add to tests/test_argument_spec.py:

class TestExampleFunctionsSerialization:
- test_scalar_functions: DoubleColumnFunction, AddColumnsFunction, UpperCaseFunction
- test_table_functions: SequenceFunction, RangeFunction, ConstantTableFunction
- test_table_in_out_functions: EchoFunction, BufferInputFunction, RepeatInputsFunction, SumAllColumnsFunction

Each test should:
1. Call extract_argument_specs(cls)
2. Verify all specs have non-null arrow_type
3. Verify schema roundtrip works

title: Add tests for varargs metadata validation
description:
In tests/test_metadata.py:
- Varargs must be positional - error if named
- Only one varargs allowed - error if multiple
- Varargs must be last positional - error if regular arg after
- Varargs + TableInput ordering works correctly
- Arrow serialization round-trip preserves is_varargs
- ParameterInfo.is_varargs correctly extracted
close_reason: Added tests for varargs metadata extraction and validation

title: Add varargs support for trailing positional arguments
description: Allow a single trailing positional argument to accept one or more values of the same type using Arg[T](position, varargs=True). Returns tuple[T, ...]. Requires at least 1 value. Must be last positional arg (before TableInput if present).
close_reason: Completed varargs support for trailing positional arguments

Files to create:

test_serialization.py:

test_catalog_interface.py:

test_catalog_client.py:

test_integration.py:

Protocol conformance tests:

- Multi-row input batches (should fail)

title: Test SchemaValidationError detailed message paths
notes:
Coverage: 67% in vgi/exceptions.py. Missing tests for:
- Lines 116-123: Type mismatch detection in _build_detailed_message
- Lines 128-131: Field order difference detection

title: Split metadata.py Arrow serialization into separate module
description: metadata.py is 932 lines with two distinct concerns: 1) metadata resolution (enums, dataclasses, parameter extraction, resolve_metadata) and 2) Arrow serialization (schema definitions, to_arrow/from_arrow functions). Split Arrow serialization into metadata_serialization.py or metadata_arrow.py for better separation of concerns.
close_reason: Not warranted - metadata.py is well-organized with clear section headers. Arrow serialization (~165 lines) is tightly coupled to data classes (uses their to_dict/from_dict methods). Splitting would add import complexity without significant benefit.

title: Fix schema_contents() default implementation
description:
Implement the default schema_contents() method that has a FIXME comment.

File: vgi/catalog/catalog_interface.py

Current state: 
```python
def schema_contents(...) -> Iterable[TableInfo | ViewInfo | FunctionInfo]:
    # FIXME: write this implementation for the worker.
```

The default implementation should:
1. Access the Worker's registered functions
2. Convert each function's metadata to FunctionInfo
3. Return them as the contents of the 'main' schema

This requires:
- Access to Worker.functions registry
- Converting function metadata to FunctionInfo format
- May need to pass worker reference to CatalogInterface

Alternative: Just raise NotImplementedError and require subclasses to implement.

title: Abstract common worker batch processing logic
description: Worker batch processing methods _process_scalar_batches (377-466), _process_batches (468-550), and _generate_batches (552-593) share significant structure: IPC writer/reader setup, batch counting/logging, main processing loop. Extract common logic to reduce duplication - consider a BatchProcessor helper class or template method pattern.
close_reason: Analysis complete: abstraction not warranted. The three methods have sufficiently different logic (input handling, log message loops, protocol types) that abstracting them would add complexity without meaningful benefit. Current code is already readable at ~70-90 lines each.

title: Add InvocationType.CATALOG to vgi/invocation.py

Extend InvocationType enum to support catalog invocations.

Files to modify:
- vgi/invocation.py

Changes:
1. Add CATALOG = 'catalog' to InvocationType enum
2. Update docstrings to document the new type
3. Ensure serialization/deserialization handles the new value

The CATALOG invocation type indicates the function_name field contains a CatalogInterface method name (e.g., 'catalog_attach', 'schemas', 'table_get').

Test that CATALOG type serializes and deserializes correctly.

title: Extract _should_terminate into shared base class
description: Identical _should_terminate method is copy-pasted in all three function modules. Implementation is always: check if log_message exists and level is EXCEPTION. Move to shared base class (Function or new ProcessingMixin) to eliminate duplication.
close_reason: Completed as part of PR #8 - _should_terminate moved to Function base class

title: Create example function using DuckDB settings
description:
Create an example function that demonstrates using DuckDB settings to determine its output.

Requirements:
- Function declares required_settings in Meta
- Output schema depends on a setting value (e.g., include extra column based on setting)
- Clear documentation showing the pattern

Example ideas:
1. TimezoneAwareFunction: Output includes timezone info based on 'timezone' setting
2. VerboseOutput: Adds debug columns when 'debug_mode' setting is true
3. NumericPrecision: Uses 'numeric_precision' to determine output type precision

Add to vgi/examples/ and register in ExampleWorker.

title: Test RowCountMismatchError when output exceeds input rows
notes:
Coverage: 86% in vgi/scalar_function.py. Missing tests for:
- Lines 134-142: Error message when output has MORE rows than input

Current tests cover when output < input but not output > input.
Need a test that returns an array with more elements than input rows.
close_reason: Added test for RowCountMismatchError when output exceeds input. Coverage improved from 86% to 93%.

title: Consolidate _OutputComplete classes into shared module
description: Three nearly identical _OutputComplete classes exist in scalar_function.py:168-197 (_ScalarOutputComplete), table_function.py:136-175 (_OutputComplete), and table_in_out_function.py:356-400 (_OutputComplete). All are frozen dataclasses with batch field, log_message field, and from_process_result() classmethod. Extract to shared module (e.g., vgi/protocol_types.py) with a single parameterized class.

title: Add slots=True to ArgumentSpec dataclass
description: ArgumentSpec is a frozen dataclass but doesn't use slots=True. Adding slots=True would reduce memory footprint and improve attribute access speed, which matters if many specs are created during introspection.

title: Unify ProtocolInput classes with shared base
description: ProtocolInput classes in scalar_function.py:151-166 and table_in_out_function.py:109-142 have similar structure with batch and metadata fields. The table_in_out version adds is_finalize logic. Create shared base ProtocolInput in protocol_types.py with table_in_out extending it.
close_reason: PR #9 created - unified ProtocolInput with shared base in protocol_types.py

title: VGI Catalog Interface Implementation
description:
Complete the VGI Catalog Interface implementation to enable DuckDB ATTACH support.

The CatalogInterface ABC is already implemented in vgi/catalog/catalog_interface.py.

Remaining work:
- Add serialize/deserialize methods to dataclasses
- Add InvocationType.CATALOG to protocol
- Worker integration for catalog dispatch  
- CatalogClient class (new worker per call pattern)
- Optional SQLite-based catalog storage
- Example InMemoryCatalog
- Tests

See: catalog-plan.md

title: Implement Arrow-based argument specification serialization
description:
## Overview

Implement serialization and deserialization of function argument specifications using Apache Arrow schemas. This enables functions to describe their argument signatures (types, positions, special markers) in a format that can be transmitted over IPC and understood by DuckDB for function registration.

## Design

Uses a **single Arrow schema** where:
- Positional arguments come first (field order = position index)
- Named arguments follow (marked with `vgi_arg=named` metadata)
- Special types (TableInput, AnyArrow, varargs) use field metadata markers

## Key Components

1. `ArgumentSpec` dataclass - represents one argument's specification
2. `argument_specs_to_schema()` - convert specs to Arrow schema
3. `schema_to_argument_specs()` - convert schema back to specs
4. `extract_argument_specs()` - extract specs from function class Arg descriptors

## Metadata Keys

| Key | Value | Meaning |
|-----|-------|---------|
| `vgi_arg` | `named` | Named argument (not positional) |
| `vgi_type` | `table` | Receives table input (Arg[TableInput]) |
| `vgi_type` | `any` | Accepts any Arrow type (Arg[AnyArrow]) |
| `vgi_varargs` | `true` | Collects remaining positional args |

## References

- Plan file: `.claude/plans/purrfect-foraging-nygaard.md`
- Arguments module: `vgi/arguments.py`
close_reason: Implemented Arrow-based argument specification serialization with tests and documentation

title: Add catalog dispatch to Worker class
description:
Integrate CatalogInterface handling into Worker class.

File: vgi/worker.py

Changes:
1. Add catalog_interface class attribute: type[CatalogInterface] | None = None

2. In run() method, detect InvocationType.CATALOG and dispatch to _handle_catalog_invocation()

3. Implement _handle_catalog_invocation(invocation: Invocation):
   - Check catalog_interface is not None (raise ValueError if missing)
   - Instantiate catalog_interface class
   - Get method from function_name field (e.g., 'catalog_attach')
   - Deserialize arguments from input batch (column names → kwargs)
   - Call method with keyword arguments
   - Serialize and stream result back

4. Key protocol difference: No bind→init→stream phases, just invoke→stream

5. Handle different return types:
   - None → 0-row/0-column batch
   - Dataclass → serialize to single-row batch
   - Iterable → stream multiple batches

6. Error handling: Return exceptions as EXCEPTION log messages (same as functions)

title: VGI Catalog Interface Implementation
description:
Add CatalogInterface ABC that lets VGI workers expose catalogs (databases), schemas, tables, views, and functions. Enables DuckDB ATTACH command support via VGI workers.

Key components:
- Type aliases and dataclasses (AttachId, TransactionId, SchemaInfo, TableInfo, etc.)
- CatalogInterface abstract base class with ~40 methods
- InvocationType.CATALOG for protocol dispatch
- CatalogClient for client-side operations
- Worker integration for catalog invocation handling

See: catalog-plan.md and ~/.claude/plans/iterative-waddling-adleman.md

Example usage in function:

    # or

title: Design: DuckDB settings/pragmas access for VGI functions
description:
Design how VGI functions can declare required DuckDB settings/pragmas in their Meta class, and how these settings values should be passed during the bind phase.

Key design decisions:
1. How to declare required settings in function Meta (e.g., required_settings = ['timezone', 'threads'])
2. How to add settings to Invocation dataclass
3. How settings values should be accessed in function code
4. Serialization format for settings in Arrow IPC

Recommendation: Add 'duckdb_settings: dict[str, str] | None' to Invocation and 'required_settings: list[str]' to Meta class.

title: Update vgi/__init__.py with argument_spec exports
description:
## Overview

Update the main package `__init__.py` to export the new argument specification classes and functions.

## File Location

`vgi/__init__.py`

## Changes Required

### Add Import Statement

Add import from the new module:

```python
from vgi.argument_spec import (
    ArgumentSpec,
    argument_specs_to_schema,
    schema_to_argument_specs,
)
```

### Update __all__ List

Add the new exports to `__all__`:

```python
__all__ = [
    # ... existing exports ...
    "ArgumentSpec",
    "argument_specs_to_schema",
    "schema_to_argument_specs",
]
```

### Placement

- Import should be grouped with other argument-related imports (near `Arg`, `Arguments`)
- Exports in `__all__` should be alphabetically sorted

## Verification

After changes, verify:
1. `from vgi import ArgumentSpec` works
2. `from vgi import argument_specs_to_schema` works
3. `from vgi import schema_to_argument_specs` works

description:
Update vgi/worker.py to process DuckDB settings from Invocation during the bind phase.

Changes needed:

- Validate that all required_settings (from Meta) are present in invocation
- Pass settings to function instance for access
- Log settings usage for debugging

The worker should validate settings early in bind to fail fast if required settings are missing.

title: Create vgi/argument_spec.py module
description:
## Overview

Create the core module implementing Arrow-based argument specification serialization.

## File Location

`vgi/argument_spec.py`

## Constants to Define

```python
# Metadata keys (all bytes for Arrow compatibility)
VGI_ARG_KEY = b"vgi_arg"
VGI_ARG_NAMED = b"named"

VGI_TYPE_KEY = b"vgi_type"
VGI_TYPE_TABLE = b"table"
VGI_TYPE_ANY = b"any"

VGI_VARARGS_KEY = b"vgi_varargs"
VGI_VARARGS_TRUE = b"true"
```

## ArgumentSpec Dataclass

```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ArgumentSpec:
    """Specification for a single function argument."""
    name: str                      # Python attribute name
    position: int | str            # int for positional index, str for named key
    arrow_type: pa.DataType        # Arrow type (pa.null() for special types)
    is_table_input: bool = False   # Arg[TableInput]
    is_any_type: bool = False      # Arg[AnyArrow]
    is_varargs: bool = False       # varargs=True
```

## Functions to Implement

### argument_specs_to_schema(specs: Sequence[ArgumentSpec]) -> pa.Schema

Convert ArgumentSpecs to a single Arrow schema:
1. Sort specs: positional first (by index), then named
2. For each spec, create a pa.field with:
   - name = spec.name
   - type = spec.arrow_type (or pa.null() for table/any)
   - metadata = appropriate markers based on flags
3. Return pa.schema(fields)

### schema_to_argument_specs(schema: pa.Schema) -> list[ArgumentSpec]

Convert schema back to ArgumentSpecs:
1. Iterate through schema fields in order
2. Track position index (increments for non-named args)
3. Check field metadata for markers:
   - `vgi_arg=named` -> position is field name string
   - `vgi_type=table` -> is_table_input=True
   - `vgi_type=any` -> is_any_type=True
   - `vgi_varargs=true` -> is_varargs=True
4. Return list of ArgumentSpec

### extract_argument_specs(cls: type, arg_types: dict[str, pa.DataType]) -> list[ArgumentSpec]

Extract specs from a function class with Arg descriptors:
1. Walk class MRO to find all Arg descriptors (like extract_parameters in metadata.py)
2. For each Arg descriptor:
   - Get name from attribute name
   - Get position from arg.position
   - Get arrow_type from arg_types dict
   - Check type hints for TableInput/AnyArrow
   - Check arg.varargs flag
3. Sort and return list

## Dependencies

- Import `Arg`, `TableInput`, `AnyArrow` from `vgi.arguments`
- Reference `extract_parameters()` pattern in `vgi/metadata.py`
close_reason: Created vgi/argument_spec.py with ArgumentSpec dataclass and serialization functions

title: Create docs/argument-serialization.md
description:
## Overview

Create LLM-friendly documentation explaining the argument specification serialization format. This document should enable future implementors (human or AI) to understand how function argument signatures are serialized to Arrow schemas.

## File Location

`docs/argument-serialization.md`

## Document Structure

### Title and Purpose

Explain that this document describes how VGI function argument specifications are serialized to Apache Arrow schemas for IPC transmission and DuckDB function registration.

### Quick Reference

A concise summary table showing:
- Metadata keys and their meanings
- Special type representations

### Schema Format

Explain the single-schema design:
1. All arguments are fields in one Arrow schema
2. Positional arguments come first, in order (field index = position index)
3. Named arguments follow, marked with metadata
4. Field name = Python attribute name (or argument key for named)
5. Field type = exact Arrow type

### Metadata Keys Reference

Complete table of all metadata keys:

| Key | Value | Description |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| `vgi_arg` | `named` | Field is a named argument, not positional. The field name is the argument key. |
| `vgi_type` | `table` | Argument receives streaming table input (Arg[TableInput]). Arrow type is pa.null(). |
| `vgi_type` | `any` | Argument accepts any Arrow type (Arg[AnyArrow]). Arrow type is pa.null(). |
| `vgi_varargs` | `true` | Argument collects all remaining positional args. Arrow type is the element type. |

### Special Type Handling

Explain how special argument types are represented:

#### TableInput
- Arrow type: `pa.null()`
- Metadata: `{b"vgi_type": b"table"}`
- Meaning: This position receives streaming RecordBatches, not a scalar value

#### AnyArrow
- Arrow type: `pa.null()`
- Metadata: `{b"vgi_type": b"any"}`
- Meaning: Accepts any valid Arrow scalar type at runtime

#### Varargs
- Arrow type: The element type (e.g., `pa.int64()` for `Arg[int](..., varargs=True)`)
- Metadata: `{b"vgi_varargs": b"true"}`
- Meaning: Collects all remaining positional arguments from this position onwards

### Examples

#### Example 1: Simple Function

```python
class MyFunction(TableInOutFunction):
    count = Arg[int](0)           # Positional 0
    name = Arg[str](1)            # Positional 1
    verbose = Arg[bool]("verbose") # Named

# Serializes to:
schema = pa.schema([
    pa.field("count", pa.int64()),
    pa.field("name", pa.utf8()),
    pa.field("verbose", pa.bool_(), metadata={b"vgi_arg": b"named"}),
])
```

#### Example 2: Function with Table Input

```python
class TransformFunction(TableInOutFunction):
    multiplier = Arg[float](0)
    data = Arg[TableInput](1)

# Serializes to:
schema = pa.schema([
    pa.field("multiplier", pa.float64()),
    pa.field("data", pa.null(), metadata={b"vgi_type": b"table"}),
])
```

#### Example 3: Function with Varargs

```python
class SumFunction(TableInOutFunction):
    columns = Arg[str](0, varargs=True)

# Serializes to:
schema = pa.schema([
    pa.field("columns", pa.utf8(), metadata={b"vgi_varargs": b"true"}),
])
```

#### Example 4: Complex Function

```python
class ComplexFunction(TableInOutFunction):
    count = Arg[int](0)
    data = Arg[TableInput](1)
    extra = Arg[float](2, varargs=True)
    format = Arg[str]("format")
    threshold = Arg[AnyArrow]("threshold")

# Serializes to:
schema = pa.schema([
    pa.field("count", pa.int64()),
    pa.field("data", pa.null(), metadata={b"vgi_type": b"table"}),
    pa.field("extra", pa.float64(), metadata={b"vgi_varargs": b"true"}),
    pa.field("format", pa.utf8(), metadata={b"vgi_arg": b"named"}),
    pa.field("threshold", pa.null(), metadata={b"vgi_arg": b"named", b"vgi_type": b"any"}),
])
```

### Serialization Code

Show how to serialize and deserialize:

```python
# Serialize to bytes
schema_bytes = schema.serialize().to_pybytes()

# Deserialize from bytes
schema = pa.ipc.read_schema(pa.py_buffer(schema_bytes))
```

### Parsing Algorithm

Explain how to parse a schema back to argument specs:

1. Initialize position_index = 0
2. For each field in schema:
   a. Check if field has `vgi_arg=named` metadata
   b. If named: position = field.name (string)
   c. If positional: position = position_index, then increment position_index
   d. Check for `vgi_type` metadata (table or any)
   e. Check for `vgi_varargs` metadata
   f. Create ArgumentSpec with extracted info

### Not Included

Explicitly state what is NOT serialized:
- Default values
- Validation constraints (ge, le, choices, pattern)
- Documentation strings

These are Python-side concerns handled by the Arg descriptor at runtime.

title: Add arrow_type parameter to Arg class
description:

title: Complete ReadOnlyCatalogInterface implementation
description:
Complete ReadOnlyCatalogInterface that prevents all DDL operations.

File: vgi/catalog/catalog_interface.py (already exists)

Current state: Only has class attributes, no method overrides.

Changes needed:
1. Override all DDL methods to raise CatalogReadOnlyError:
   - catalog_create, catalog_drop
   - schema_create, schema_drop
   - table_create, table_drop, table_rename, table_comment_set
   - All table_column_* methods
   - table_not_null_set, table_not_null_drop
   - view_create, view_drop, view_rename, view_comment_set
   - Transaction methods (begin/commit/rollback)

2. Create CatalogReadOnlyError exception in vgi/exceptions.py

3. Ensure catalog_attach returns supports_transactions=False

Include tests verifying all DDL operations raise CatalogReadOnlyError.

title: Create vgi/catalog/client.py - CatalogClient class
description:
Create CatalogClient for client-side catalog operations.

- start() / stop() methods

Core methods (mirroring CatalogInterface):
- catalogs() -> list[str]
- attach(name, options) -> CatalogAttachResult

- view_get(...) -> ViewInfo | None

Handle:

- Exception propagation from worker

title: Unify ProtocolOutput classes with shared base
description: ProtocolOutput classes in table_function.py:177-224 and table_in_out_function.py:144-207 share similar metadata() method and from_process_result() classmethod. The table_in_out version adds status field. Create shared base with table_in_out extending it for status support.
close_reason: Not warranted - dataclass inheritance with slots=True doesn't allow adding required field (status) between inherited fields. The classes have different semantics (table_in_out requires status for generator state tracking) making inheritance impractical.

title: Create InMemoryCatalog example implementation
description:
Create an in-memory catalog implementation for testing and as an example.

File: vgi/examples/catalog.py

InMemoryCatalog(CatalogInterface):
- In-memory storage using dicts

- Does NOT support transactions (returns None)

Data structures:

Create example worker:
```python
class InMemoryCatalogWorker(Worker):

```

Add entry point: vgi-example-catalog-worker

title: Create vgi/catalog/storage.py - Catalog persistence
description:
Create storage layer for catalog attach_id and transaction_id persistence.

Files to create:
- vgi/catalog/storage.py

CatalogStorage protocol (similar to FunctionStorage):
- attach_put(attach_id, catalog_name, options) -> None
- attach_get(attach_id) -> tuple[str, dict] | None
- attach_delete(attach_id) -> None
- attach_list() -> list[AttachId]

- transaction_put(transaction_id, attach_id, state) -> None
- transaction_get(transaction_id) -> tuple[AttachId, bytes] | None
- transaction_delete(transaction_id) -> None

CatalogStorageSqlite implementation:
- Default location: ~/.state/vgi/vgi_catalog.db
- WAL mode for concurrent access
- Schema:
  CREATE TABLE catalog_attachments (
    attach_id BLOB PRIMARY KEY,
    catalog_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    options TEXT,  -- JSON
    created_at REAL DEFAULT (julianday('now'))
  )
  CREATE TABLE catalog_transactions (
    transaction_id BLOB PRIMARY KEY,
    attach_id BLOB NOT NULL,
    state BLOB,
    created_at REAL DEFAULT (julianday('now'))
  )

Include cleanup strategies for stale attachments/transactions.

title: Create vgi/catalog/serialization.py - Arrow serialization
description:
Create Arrow IPC serialization for all catalog types.

Files to create:
- vgi/catalog/serialization.py

Arrow schemas for:
- CatalogAttachResult: attach_id, supports_transactions, supports_time_travel, catalog_version_frozen, catalog_version
- SchemaInfo: attach_id, name, is_default, comment, tags
- TableInfo: name, schema_name, columns, primary_key_columns, not_null_constraints, unique_constraints, check_constraints, comment, tags
- ViewInfo: name, schema_name, definition, comment, tags
- FunctionInfo: name, schema_name, function_type, arguments, output_schema, comment, tags
- ScanFunctionResult: function_name, max_processes, invocation_id

Functions:
- serialize_<type>() -> bytes for each type
- deserialize_<type>(batch) -> Type for each type
- Arrow schema constants for each type

Serialization convention:
- Single-row batches for scalar returns
- Multi-row batches for streaming (Iterable returns)
- None = 0-row/0-column batch
- Empty list = 0-row batch with schema

Include round-trip serialization tests for all types.

title: Use sentinel type pattern instead of Any for _MISSING in arguments.py
notes:
Line 33: _MISSING: Any = object()

Replace with proper sentinel type pattern:
```python
from typing import Final

class _Missing:
    __slots__ = ()
    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return '<MISSING>'

MISSING: Final = _Missing()
```

This removes 1 Any and provides better type safety for default value checking.
Part of 26.89% imprecision in arguments.py (59 Anys total).
close_reason: Replaced _MISSING: Any = object() with proper _MissingType sentinel class. Improves type safety and removes 1 Any.

title: Add validation for contiguous positional indices
description: Neither argument_specs_to_schema() nor schema_to_argument_specs() validates that positional argument indices are contiguous (0, 1, 2...). Gaps like (0, 2, 3) would serialize fine but might indicate a bug. Consider adding validation that positional indices form a contiguous sequence starting from 0.

title: Create vgi/catalog/catalog_interface.py - CatalogInterface ABC
description:
Create the CatalogInterface abstract base class.

Abstract methods (@abstractmethod - must implement):

- view_get(attach_id, transaction_id, schema_name, name) -> ViewInfo | None

Optional methods (default raises NotImplementedError):

title: Add required_settings to function Meta class

Update function metadata to support declaring required DuckDB settings.

class MyFunction(TableInOutFunction):

title: Update client to pass DuckDB settings in Invocation
description:
Update vgi/client/client.py to support passing DuckDB settings.

Changes needed:
- Add 'duckdb_settings: dict[str, str] | None = None' parameter to relevant methods
- Include settings in Invocation creation

The client needs to know what settings to pass. Options:
1. Client queries worker for function metadata first
2. Settings passed explicitly by caller
3. Client introspects function class if available locally

title: Investigate named argument field name ambiguity
description: For named arguments, position is set to field.name (the SQL key). But there's potential ambiguity between the Python attribute name and the named argument key if they could differ. Currently they're the same, but if ArgumentSpec.name (attribute) ever differs from ArgumentSpec.position (key), the schema only preserves one. Investigate whether this is a real concern or document the assumption that they're always equal.

title: Add protocol types for IPC stream writers in cli.py
notes:
Line 53: self._writer: Any = None

Could define a Protocol type for the IPC stream writer interface:
```python
class IPCWriter(Protocol):
    def write_batch(self, batch: pa.RecordBatch) -> None: ...
    def close(self) -> None: ...
```

Part of 14.17% imprecision in cli.py (34 Anys total).
close_reason: Replaced _writer: Any with _writer: pq.ParquetWriter | None. Removes 1 Any and provides proper type information.

title: Create tests/test_argument_spec.py
description:
## Overview

Create comprehensive tests for the argument specification serialization module.

## File Location

`tests/test_argument_spec.py`

## Test Classes and Cases

### TestArgumentSpecToSchema

Test converting ArgumentSpec objects to Arrow schema.

#### test_positional_arguments_preserve_order
- Create specs with positions 0, 1, 2
- Convert to schema
- Verify field order matches position order
- Verify field types are preserved

#### test_named_arguments_have_metadata
- Create spec with position='key' (named)
- Convert to schema
- Verify field has `vgi_arg=named` metadata

#### test_mixed_positional_and_named
- Create mix of positional (0, 1) and named ('format', 'verbose') specs
- Convert to schema
- Verify positional come first, then named
- Verify named have correct metadata

#### test_table_input_uses_null_type
- Create spec with is_table_input=True
- Convert to schema
- Verify field type is pa.null()
- Verify field has `vgi_type=table` metadata

#### test_any_type_uses_null_type
- Create spec with is_any_type=True
- Convert to schema
- Verify field type is pa.null()
- Verify field has `vgi_type=any` metadata

#### test_varargs_has_metadata
- Create spec with is_varargs=True and arrow_type=pa.int64()
- Convert to schema
- Verify field type is pa.int64() (element type preserved)
- Verify field has `vgi_varargs=true` metadata

### TestSchemaToArgumentSpecs

Test converting Arrow schema back to ArgumentSpec objects.

#### test_positional_arguments_from_schema
- Create schema with 3 fields (no metadata)
- Convert to specs
- Verify positions are 0, 1, 2

#### test_named_arguments_from_metadata
- Create schema with `vgi_arg=named` metadata on fields
- Convert to specs
- Verify position is field name string

#### test_table_input_detected
- Create schema with `vgi_type=table` metadata
- Convert to specs
- Verify is_table_input=True

#### test_any_type_detected
- Create schema with `vgi_type=any` metadata
- Convert to specs
- Verify is_any_type=True

#### test_varargs_detected
- Create schema with `vgi_varargs=true` metadata
- Convert to specs
- Verify is_varargs=True

### TestRoundTrip

Test that specs survive serialization round-trip.

#### test_complex_arrow_types_preserved
Test each of these types round-trips correctly:
- pa.int64(), pa.float32(), pa.utf8()
- pa.list_(pa.float64())
- pa.struct([pa.field('a', pa.int32()), pa.field('b', pa.string())])
- pa.map_(pa.string(), pa.int64())
- pa.decimal128(10, 2)
- pa.timestamp('us', tz='UTC')

#### test_full_function_signature_roundtrip
- Create specs matching a realistic function:
  - count: int, position 0
  - data: TableInput, position 1
  - extra: float varargs, position 2
  - format: str, named 'format'
- Convert to schema, serialize to bytes, deserialize, convert back to specs
- Verify all specs match original

### TestExtractArgumentSpecs

Test extracting specs from function classes.

#### test_extract_from_simple_function
- Define function class with Arg descriptors
- Call extract_argument_specs with arg_types dict
- Verify specs match descriptors

#### test_extract_table_input
- Define function with Arg[TableInput]
- Extract specs
- Verify is_table_input=True

#### test_extract_any_arrow
- Define function with Arg[AnyArrow]
- Extract specs
- Verify is_any_type=True

#### test_extract_varargs
- Define function with Arg[int](2, varargs=True)
- Extract specs
- Verify is_varargs=True

### TestEdgeCases

#### test_empty_schema
- Convert empty list of specs to schema
- Verify empty schema works
- Convert back, verify empty list

#### test_only_named_arguments
- Create specs with only named arguments (no positional)
- Round-trip and verify

#### test_only_positional_arguments
- Create specs with only positional arguments (no named)
- Round-trip and verify

## Test Utilities

Consider creating fixtures for common patterns:
- `make_spec()` helper for creating ArgumentSpec
- Sample function classes for extraction tests
close_reason: Created comprehensive tests with 43 passing test cases

title: Update example functions with explicit arrow_type

Most examples keep Arg[int](0) to test auto-inference.

InMemoryCatalog(CatalogInterface):

- Generates attach_id as random UUID bytes

Data structures:

This makes the API more consistent since max_processes is effectively a constant per function class and properties are more idiomatic for such values.

- catalog_create, catalog_drop
- schema_create, schema_drop
- All table_* DDL methods
- All view_* DDL methods
- Transaction methods (optional - could allow read-only transactions)

Properties:
- supports_transactions = False (class attribute)

title: Add InvocationType.CATALOG to protocol

Extend InvocationType enum to support catalog invocations.

1. Add CATALOG = 'catalog' to InvocationType enum

The CATALOG invocation type indicates:
- function_name field contains a CatalogInterface method name (e.g., 'catalog_attach', 'schemas', 'table_get')
- Simplified protocol: invoke → stream (no bind→init→stream phases)
- Input batch has exactly 1 row with column names matching method parameters

Ensure existing serialization/deserialization handles the new value.

title: Implement optional CatalogStorage with SQLite default
description:
Create optional storage layer for catalog attach_id and transaction_id persistence.

File: vgi/catalog/storage.py

CatalogStorage protocol:
- attach_put(attach_id, catalog_name, options) -> None
- attach_get(attach_id) -> tuple[str, dict] | None
- attach_delete(attach_id) -> None
- attach_list() -> list[AttachId]
- transaction_put(transaction_id, attach_id, state) -> None
- transaction_get(transaction_id) -> tuple[AttachId, bytes] | None
- transaction_delete(transaction_id) -> None

CatalogStorageSqlite implementation:
- Default location: ~/.state/vgi/vgi_catalog.db
- WAL mode for concurrent access
- Similar pattern to FunctionStorageSqlite

Usage:
- CatalogInterface subclasses can optionally use storage
- Simple catalogs can ignore (return empty attach_id bytes)
- Catalogs needing persistence override storage attribute

Add storage class attribute to CatalogInterface with None default.

title: Test FunctionStorageSqlite: global_delete, global_exists, queue_clear

- Lines 273-278: global_delete method
- Lines 282-290: global_exists method

- Lines 376-385: queue_clear method

These storage operations need direct unit tests to ensure correctness.
close_reason: Added comprehensive tests for FunctionStorageSqlite. Coverage improved from 83% to 98%.

title: Consolidate test client infrastructure in testing.py
description: testing.py has three test client classes (FunctionTestClient, TableFunctionTestClient, ScalarFunctionTestClient) with shared infrastructure patterns. Extend _BaseTestClient pattern to reduce code duplication. Consider using a single unified client with method dispatch based on function type.
close_reason: Not warranted - _BaseTestClient already provides shared infrastructure (context manager, log capture, logging). The three clients handle genuinely different protocols (TableInOut with finalize, TableFunction with no input, Scalar with different protocol). Unifying would add type detection complexity without real benefit.

title: Improve type annotations in testing.py test helpers
notes:
92.61% type coverage (70 Anys) in vgi/testing.py

Main opportunities:

  Could use ParamSpec or more specific signatures
- Lines 151-152: `positional: tuple[pa.Scalar[Any], ...]` - unavoidable (PyArrow)
- Lines 761, 843: Log expectation dicts - could use TypedDict

Lower priority since these are test helpers and flexibility is intentional.

title: Add validation for missing arg_types in extract_argument_specs
description: In extract_argument_specs(), if an argument name is missing from arg_types dict, it silently defaults to pa.null(). This could mask bugs where the caller forgot to provide a type. Consider raising an error or logging a warning when a type mapping is missing.

title: Add tests for varargs in Arg descriptor
description:
In tests/test_arguments.py:
- Basic varargs: receives multiple values as tuple
- Single value varargs: works with exactly 1 value  
- Empty varargs: raises error when 0 values
- Type validation: each element validated
- Varargs with constraints (ge, le, etc.): validates each element
- Varargs with choices: validates each element against choices
close_reason: Added comprehensive tests for varargs in Arg descriptor and Arguments.get_varargs()

title: Add catalog dispatch to vgi/worker.py
description:
Integrate CatalogInterface handling into Worker class.

Files to modify:
- vgi/worker.py

Changes:
1. Add catalog_interface class attribute: type[CatalogInterface] | None = None

2. In run() method, detect InvocationType.CATALOG and dispatch to _handle_catalog_invocation()

3. Implement _handle_catalog_invocation(invocation: Invocation):
   - Check catalog_interface is not None (raise ValueError if missing)
   - Instantiate catalog_interface class
   - Get method from function_name field (e.g., 'catalog_attach')
   - Deserialize arguments from invocation
   - Call method with keyword arguments
   - Stream result back (single batch for scalars, multiple for iterables)

4. Implement _deserialize_catalog_args(invocation, method) -> dict:
   - Extract arguments from invocation batch
   - Match column names to method parameter names
   - Handle optional parameters (nullable columns)

5. Implement _stream_catalog_result(result):
   - If None: send 0-row/0-column batch
   - If dataclass: serialize and send single-row batch
   - If Iterable: stream multiple batches

Key difference from functions: No bind→init→stream phases, just invoke→stream.

Include error handling that returns exceptions as EXCEPTION log messages.

title: Update documentation for DuckDB settings feature
description:
Update documentation to cover DuckDB settings feature.

2. docs/metadata.md - Document required_settings in Meta class
3. CLAUDE.md - Add example showing settings usage pattern
4. docs/lifecycle.md - Mention settings availability during bind

Include:
- When settings are available (bind phase and later)
- How to declare required settings
- How to access settings in function code
- Example patterns for settings-dependent output schemas
close_reason: Documentation updated in protocol.md and CLAUDE.md

title: Export metadata constants in argument_spec __all__
description: The metadata constants (VGI_ARG_KEY, VGI_ARG_NAMED, VGI_TYPE_KEY, VGI_TYPE_TABLE, VGI_TYPE_ANY, VGI_VARARGS_KEY, VGI_VARARGS_TRUE) are not exported in __all__. If external code needs to parse schemas, they'd need to hardcode these values. Add them to __all__ and re-export from vgi/__init__.py.

title: Add tests for AnyValue argument type
description: Test AnyValue with various Arrow types (int, string, float, etc.) and ensure proper metadata extraction

title: Write end-to-end tests for DuckDB settings feature
description:
Create comprehensive tests for the DuckDB settings feature.

Test scenarios:
1. Function with required_settings receives settings correctly
2. Function with no required_settings works without settings
3. Missing required setting fails with clear error
4. Setting value correctly affects output schema (bind phase)
5. Setting value correctly affects function behavior (processing phase)
6. Settings serialization/deserialization roundtrip
7. Parallel workers all receive same settings

Add tests to tests/ directory covering:
- Unit tests for Invocation serialization with settings
- Unit tests for Meta required_settings
- Integration tests using ExampleWorker with settings-dependent function

title: Unify storage protocols into single FunctionStorage interface
description:
## Problem

Currently there are two separate storage protocols (InitStorage and WorkerStateStorage) with inconsistent naming:
- `create` vs `store` (different verbs for similar operations)
- `collect_and_delete` is verbose and describes implementation

Users wanting a custom storage backend (Redis, DynamoDB, etc.) must implement two separate classes.

## Solution

Unify into a single `FunctionStorage` protocol with consistent naming using prefixes to group related methods:

```python
class FunctionStorage(Protocol):
    """Storage protocol for VGI distributed function execution.
    
    Three access patterns:
    - Global state: Init data shared across all workers (key-value with auto-generated keys)
    - Worker state: Partial results per worker (collected during finalization)
    - Work queue: Atomic work distribution across workers (FIFO queue)
    """
    
    # Global state (init data)
    def global_put(self, value: bytes) -> bytes: ...  # Returns auto-generated key
    def global_get(self, key: bytes) -> bytes: ...
    def global_delete(self, key: bytes) -> None: ...
    def global_exists(self, key: bytes) -> bool: ...
    
    # Worker state (partial results per worker)
    def worker_put(self, invocation_id: bytes, worker_id: int, state: bytes) -> None: ...
    def worker_collect(self, invocation_id: bytes) -> list[bytes]: ...  # Atomic collect+delete
    
    # Work queue (distributed work items)
    def queue_push(self, invocation_id: bytes, items: list[bytes]) -> int: ...
    def queue_pop(self, invocation_id: bytes) -> bytes | None: ...  # Atomic claim
    def queue_clear(self, invocation_id: bytes) -> int: ...
```

## Design Rationale

- **Prefixes** (`global_`, `worker_`, `queue_`): Clear grouping, good autocomplete
- **Consistent verbs**: `put/get` for storage, `push/pop` for queue
- **Minimal interface**: 9 methods total (down from 9, but now unified)
- **Single class variable** in Function: `storage: ClassVar[FunctionStorage]`

## Files to Change

- `vgi/function_storage.py`: New protocol + merged FunctionStorageSqlite
- `vgi/function.py`: Single `storage` class variable, update all method calls
